entry.title: Re: CAP with Fiori Elements: Actions on List Report / Object Page using Annotations – Part2
entry.content:
@jgonzal I am glad that it helped you.  

Coming to your question, it should be possible as long as external app works on routing based page loading. (Fiori Elements works)

entry.title: Re: BOM Components consumption with PI Sheet
entry.content:
Hello

This link from SAP Help might be useful to you:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ERP/c067fd920d734e00ad38f4f6f4436b0a/f787bf53f106b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US&version=6.05.latest

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Why do we get "Exception "ERROR_AT_SAVE" triggered (conversion object ) when Mig Object Routing ?
entry.content:
The Migration Object Routing in S/4HANA Cloud fails with error ERROR_AT_SAVE

What is the cause and how do we prevent it from occurring ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Why do we get "Exception "ERROR_AT_SAVE" triggered (c...
entry.content:
The error is raised if the BADI BOM_BEFORE_SAVE is active ( Business Context : Master Data:Bill of Material Header or Bill of Material Item )

In this BADI there is a custom validation check performed at save that raises an error .

The BOM BADI is called in the Migration Object Routing as Component Allocation is involved.

Please adjust the code in BADI implementation / deactivate if necessary so the custom validation will not be performed.

Please consider implementing a check on the System Variable SY-UNAME in the BADI implementation for whoever is running the Data Migration to skip the custom code

This is performed at the Customers on risk.

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Quantity confirmation with different User ID...
entry.content: Thank you for your quick reply Manoel. I understand it might be tricky to combine all information finally in one report. Just one more question. Where can I find the information which I enter in the fiel Custom Field 1 Label. In your example "MANOELCOSTA". I replicated the solution but neither in the production report nor in the PSN report I can see the information.

entry.title: Re: ASM commerceservices extension/plug in, Hybris
entry.content:
Hi there, 

Customer support agents (CSAs) can use the Assisted Service Module (ASM) to assign anonymous storefront carts to existing customer accounts. Please refer the below documentation

Binding an Anonymous Cart to a Customer Account

Hope this would answer your question

Thanks and regards, 
Pradeep Pai Karkala
SAP Product Support

Hi Omkar, 

please check the documentation: 

Creating a B2B Customer

Hope this would answer your question

Thanks and regards, 
Pradeep Pai Karkala 
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: How can I disable Swagger in Production but enable Sw...
entry.content:
Unfortunately, you cannot directly configure Swagger UI via manifest.json file in SAP commerce cloud . You can only integrate it with swaggerintegration. There is no other approach as of now. 

Thanks and regards, 
Pradeep Pai Karkala 
SAP CX Product Support

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 0

entry.title: HIRING ALERT
entry.content: Any IS-Media professional looking for a new opportunity. Please contact me!!

entry.title: Re: SAP Developer Challenge - APIs - Task 0 - Learn to share your task results
Applying % by month in IncrPct measure to Amount measure by Product in SAC
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

My model has dimensions: Version(Plan), Date(202301 to 202512), Product (#, P1, P2, P3), DataSource(#,Input, Calc), Measures: IncrPct, Amount. 

I have two stories, one story to capture % changes against a product # and another story to see the increase in amounts by product (% applies to all products for their respective amounts). 

The current Data Action written is applying the % changes for every click on the amounts.

Let's say 10%, is applied on amount: $1000. First click is giving me $1100, second click is giving me $1210, etc. Instead what we want is that it should stay at $1100, until we publish the data. So, thought that using Datasource, we can segregate the data between what is original input values and what is calculated. 

So, we would like to apply the % change on the original data, by storing that against DataSource: 'Input'  and keep all the increments done through DataAction in the DataSource: 'Calc' only, until published, and show the values together combined in the story. Once published the data, the data against the DataSource 'Calc' needs to be merged together with the DataSource: 'Input' and all the data together merged and stored against datasource: 'Input'. 

In between, the initial data and publishing the data, all the direct inputs in the story, should always be stored against datasource: 'Input' and should be included for calculation of DataAction. 

How to achieve this?

Thanks,

SP10

entry.title: Hiring - SAP Solutions Analyst
entry.content:
MMD Services is hiring a SAP Solutions Analyst (TM) for our client - must be willing to go onsite in the Chicagoland area twice a month.

The right candidate for this role will be highly experienced with SAP S4/HANA Transportation Management (TM), coinciding modules, and confident working in a collaborative setting. Please reach out to me directly if you are interested or know anyone who might be! My email is rhorneman@mmdtech.com

entry.title: Re: I am not able to connect to Enable Now Manager from S...
entry.content: Sorry, I will upload screenshot on monday

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK application login with ADFS in internal network
entry.content: When AllowCerts is used the client uses a Safari view for the login. Most likely the IdP is caching a login cookie in Safari. If you clear the cache in Safari after logout I suspect it will then allow you to login as a new user.

entry.title: Re: Navigation menu color
entry.content: The background color is not applied, even though this is done several times

entry.title: Re: ILM Store Troubleshooting
entry.content:
any tip on

We get the following error in ILMSTOREADM

498: Parameter is missing

CX_XADK_DAS_CLIENT

DA Service Message: _DEFINE_ARCHIVE_STORES#x20;STORE_TYPE#x20;from&#x20 ;request header

entry.title: Re: SAP ACTIVATE OVERVIEW

I also suggest you check out the blog SAP Activate in under 9 minutes which goes a little deeper into each phase, its purpose, and key activities while still keeping it at an overview level.

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/sap-activate-in-under-9-minutes/ba-p/13569476

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA Public Cloud - How to enable calculated field...
entry.content:
HI,

 

Please see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/d72a13f700de4b2fbd33a6e7fc562fa9.html#enabling-calculated-fields for help on how to enable the calculated fields. It is tab on the custom fields app under data source extension.

Regards,

Dennis

entry.title: Common Master data and Master data for access control, access analysis in IAG

I have most of the configuration but am stuck at maintenance of common master data and master data separately for access analysis, access control and PAM. I am not sure where do I have to maintain that data. Any help will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 0
entry.content: Hi, I can't see my user name in that list, that means that my answer was incorrect?

entry.title: Government SEFAZ Technical Notes with Optional Events
entry.content:
Hello,

In recent years, the Secretaria da Fazenda (SEFAZ) has published several technical notes containing optional events. This means that taxpayers are not obligated to issue these events, and it is up to each company to decide whether to use them.

Since these events are optional, SAP considers the events as new features and not mandatory legal changes. Therefore, optional technical notes will not appear in the Regulatory Change Manager (RCM) tool.

You can check the following technical notes with optional events published by SEFAZ:

  • Nota Técnica NF-e 2020.007 – Evento Ator Interessado na NF-e (NF-e Intestered Actor)
  • Nota Técnica NF-e 2021.001 – Evento Comprovante de Entrega na NF-e (NF-e Delivery Confirmation)
  • Nota Técnica NF-e 2023.005 – Evento Insucesso de Entrega na NF-e (NF-e Delivery Failure)
  • Nota Técnica CT-e 2019.001 – Evento Comprovante de Entrega no CT-e (CT-e Delivery Confirmation)
  • Nota Técnica CT-e 2023.002 – Evento Insucesso de Entrega no CT-e (CT-e Delivery Failure)

SAP is still analyzing the demand for each technical note. Once planned by SAP, it will be published on the SAP Road Map Explorer for the SAP Document and Reporting, inbound and outbound invoicing options for Brazil.

Thank you,

Cláudia Wada

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK application login with ADFS in internal network
entry.content: Now it works..I can login with the popup that comes from ADFS and I can logout by deleting cache of domain "ondemand.com" from Safari browser. Thank you!!

entry.title: Re: Sap Fiori Element Composition of one issues when usin...
entry.content:
Ideally i am not sure why you need GroupVersion entity. That is the reason, pagemap can not help. However, you can still add following annotations manually in annotations.cds :

annotate service.MachineDimensionLimits with @(
    UI.LineItem #dimensionLimit : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : limitType.description,
            Label : 'Limit Type',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : min,
            Label : 'Min',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : max,
            Label : 'Max',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : unit,
            Label : 'Unit',
        },]
);

annotate service.GroupVersion with @(
    UI.Facets : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : 'General Version',
            ID : 'GeneralVersion',
            Target : '@UI.FieldGroup#GeneralVersion',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Target : 'limitsGroupVersion/dimensionLimit/@UI.LineItem#dimensionLimit',
            Label : 'Dimension Limits',
            ID : 'DimensionLimits',
        }
    ],
    UI.FieldGroup #GeneralVersion : {
        $Type : 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : limitsGroupVersion.value1,
                Label : 'value1',
            },
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : limitsGroupVersion.value2,
                Label : 'value2',
            }
        ],
    }
);

entry.title: How can I extend the number of characters of the batch number?
entry.content:
We need to extend the number of characters of the batch number.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can I extend the number of characters of the batc...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please take a look at the following note:

  • 3083930 - Length of batch number is limited to 10 characters in S/4HANA Cloud

According to the note, it's not possible to change the batch number since the batch number field is used widely in SAP system in many application areas. Changing it will cause a big impact to SAP system.

Please consider the solution part in note 3083930 to add 'external number' to batch class and use this characteristic to represent the long batch number.

Best regards.

entry.title: BW4HANA: How to hide a measure in a query depending on which node of the hierarchy we are in.
entry.content:
Hello everyone!
I'm sharing a situation we're facing that we can't find a solution to. Any help is welcome.
Thanks in advance!

Tools:

  • BW4HANA:
  • SAP Analytics Cloud (Live Model)

Problem:

We have the Customer dimension, which includes several measures, among them, Measure X. In SAC, a table is displayed with this Customer dimension, along with Measure X and others ones. This table has a specific hierarchy created in BW with multiple levels. All the measures in this table originate at the leaf level and are then summarized at different nodes levels within the hierarchy. Due to a specific business requirement, it's necessary to hide the Measure X for certain lower levels of the hierarchy, without affecting the values of their parent nodes. Additionally, we have the constraint of not being able to use a separate hierarchy or table as per the client's request. All of this needs to coexist alongside other measures, within the same hierarchy, and using the same dimension without altering anything beyond what is necessary. This change must necessarily be made on the BW/Query side, as making it within the SAC story wouldn't be useful for us.

Objective:

To modify the behavior of Measure X for the Customer dimension, specifically for a certain hierarchy. It's necessary to hide Measure X from view at lower levels of the hierarchy without using a new table, another dimension, or a different hierarchy from the one currently used.

Visual Example:

This is the current state of the table display:

And this is the desired display:

P.S. The level 4 would refer to the leaf level of the hierarchy.

Solutions attempted:

  • BW Query Designer: An attempt was made to implement logic where each node could be enumerated according to its level, and from there, restrict Measure X at the necessary levels (levels 3 and 4). In this case, as direct access to the hierarchy and its nodes wasn't possible, some nodes could not be categorized correctly due to the structure of the hierarchy and a few other issues (e.g., some level 4 nodes have 1 child, and others have more than 1). This attempt was carried out using the LEAF function, which allows us to distinguish leaf nodes of the hierarchy. The logic consisted of summarizing this LEAF value to distinguish in each case, at which levels of the hierarchy we were.
  • SAC Story (Live Model): Making this change at this level is not useful for us. However, since we also don't have the possibility to access and formulate with the hierarchy nodes, we couldn't find a way to solve this problem within the SAC story either.
  • Customer Exit: With this approach, we found that it's possible to dynamically select the nodes in which we want to hide Measure X. However, we encountered some limitations when configuring the displayed values for parent nodes and couldn't achieve the desired result. When hiding Measure X for all child nodes of a parent node, we found that we couldn't prevent the value for the parent node from also becoming 0. Therefore, instead of only hiding the value in child nodes, it also propagates to the parent nodes (e.g., hiding all level 3 nodes results in the value not being visible or being distorted in level 2 nodes).

 

SAP BW/4HANA 
BW SAP HANA Modeling Tools (Eclipse)

entry.title: Re: Sap Fiori Element Composition of one issues when usin...
entry.content:
Ideally i am not sure why you need GroupVersion entity. That is the reason, pagemap can not help. However, you can still add following annotations manually in annotations.cds :

 

annotate service.MachineDimensionLimits with @(
    UI.LineItem #dimensionLimit : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : limitType.description,
            Label : 'Limit Type',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : min,
            Label : 'Min',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : max,
            Label : 'Max',
        },{
            $Type : 'UI.DataField',
            Value : unit,
            Label : 'Unit',
        },]
);

annotate service.GroupVersion with @(
    UI.Facets : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : 'General Version',
            ID : 'GeneralVersion',
            Target : '@UI.FieldGroup#GeneralVersion',
        },
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Target : 'limitsGroupVersion/dimensionLimit/@UI.LineItem#dimensionLimit',
            Label : 'Dimension Limits',
            ID : 'DimensionLimits',
        }
    ],
    UI.FieldGroup #GeneralVersion : {
        $Type : 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : limitsGroupVersion.value1,
                Label : 'value1',
            },
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataField',
                Value : limitsGroupVersion.value2,
                Label : 'value2',
            }
        ],
    }
);

entry.title: how to remove K stock from MIGO using 411 K movement?
entry.content:
hello,

i am having a hard time removing a K stock. our plant use MIGO to remove K stock using 411 K movement. however when i tried to remove it, it is not removing the stock. LS24 still shows the K stock along with the part number.

in order for me to create a return i need to remove the K stock. please advise next step.

entry.title: Re: Enabling Change & Deployment Management in SAP Cloud ALM for SAP Integration Suite - Cloud Integ
entry.content:
Hello @moritzgysler ,

Thank you for your response.

Based on this blog (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/transport-fiori-launchpad-sites-on-sap-btp-using-sap-cloud-transport/ba-p/13494789) for setting up CTMS for managing transports for application content, I dont think we need destinations like ContentAssemblyService and CloudIntegration right? ContentAssemblyService and CloudIntegration are needed exclusively for Integration suite related transports, right?
Basically we need destinations TransportManagementService and destinations from source to target sub accounts to be deployed?

Appreciate your time and effort.

entry.title: Table for transport costs paid for outbound deliveries.

I am looking for a table combination to list freight costs paid to logistics suppliers for outbound deliveries. I need the report to contain supplier, invoice document, amount for invoice, delivery document, sales order, customer number and name, shipping point.

Thank you very much.

entry.title: SAP Master Data Rated #1 for User Satisfaction & Market Presence by G2
entry.content:
We are thrilled to share that SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) has once again been named a Leader in the Spring 2024 G2 Grid Report for Master Data Management (MDM).

Satisfied Users

Critical to our legacy of success, SAP MDG stands at the top in terms of Market Presence in the G2 MDM category. This presence attests to not only the wide adoption of our solution, but also to our unwavering commitment to provide high-quality data governance capabilities across numerous data domains, use cases, implementation styles, and industries across the globe.

A true testament is customers' satisfaction with SAP MDG, the 100% Satisfaction score awarded us the top spot in the G2 MDM Grid. In addition, 97% of users on G2 have rated SAP 4 or 5 stars – an overall approval rating covering support, usability, functionality, administration, setup, and business relationship - that also speaks to the quality and reliability of the SAP MDG solution and services.

Steering in the Right Direction

And we continue to innovate, with 96% of G2 users echoing their belief that SAP MDG's product direction is going in the right direction. Our roadmap for MDG is driven by our customers and leading market trends in data management. We believe, SAP can provide much more value than other MDM tool providers, because we understand how master data is used in business context. We can provide master data management capabilities that truly support all domains, all industries, and all approaches - embedded into the business application landscape and end-to-end processes. And, we have proven this with SAP MDG, and we will continue to deliver on this unique promise.

Selecting SAP Master Data Governance means opting for a Leader in Master Data Management – a choice founded on superior market presence, high user satisfaction, and a clear vision of the future. Join the vast network of SAP MDG users that have established a trusted foundation of business-critical master data to support operational excellence, ensure accurate analytics and AI, and accelerate digital transformation.

Check out these resources to learn more:

  • SAP Master Data Governance on SAP.com
  • SAP Master Data Governance on G2
  • Hear Why SAP Master Data Governance is a Leader for Master Data Governance

entry.title: Web Services for Concur Expense - Updating Expense Reports and Entries
entry.content:
Introduction

Concur offers a wide variety of Expense APIs, this blog post is to help guide anyone interested in using Web Services to retrieve and/or update expense report and expense report entry data

The APIs mentioned in this blog work with SAP Concur Expense Standard and Professional Editions. The following scenarios will be explained along with examples in this post:

  1. Retrieving header data for all expense reports
  2. Retrieving expense report header data for a specific ReportID
  3. Retrieving expense entries for a specific ReportID
  4. Retrieving expense entry data for a specific Expense ID
  5. Updating expense report header data
    1. Retrieve Unique User ID
    2. Update Header Text Field
    3. Update Header Connect List Fields
  6. How to update expense report entry fields

Prior to making any of the requests below, you will need to have authenticated and have a current Access Token. If you are unfamiliar with this process, I highly suggest reviewing my colleague Tim's blog post on Authentication. I have also saved the steps in this walkthrough in a Postman Collection which you can download here .

Detailed Walkthrough

First let's get familiar with some of the key variables needed to make these requests. This blog post will walk you through how to get all of these values and I have them built into my Postman collection linked in the section above.

1. Retrieving header data for all expense reports

Before we get into updating expense reports, it might be useful to know what reports are currently in Concur and some high-level attributes like their report ID, approval or payment status, and when they were submitted. To get a response that includes report header data for all reports in Concur, you can use the following:

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/api/v3.0/expense/reports?user=ALL

 

There are a wide variety of parameters you can use to filter this request, but you should always include the "user" parameter. This page lists all available params and how they should be formatted - Expense Report V3 Parameters.

2. Retrieving expense report header data for a specific ReportID

Prior to updating expense report header data, it's helpful to see what data and fields already exist. To retrieve the expense report header data you will just need the report ID.

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/{{contextType}}/reports/{{reportID}}

 

3. Retrieving expense entries for a specific ReportID

It will also be helpful to know what entries exist on the report that you are trying to update if plan on updating entry data as well. You will notice that each expense entry has its own unique "expenseId" that will be used in the next few scenarios.

To see expense entries for a report you will need to use the following:

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/{{contextType}}/reports/{{reportID}}/expenses

 

 

*Note that each expense has its own expenseID, we will use this going forward*

4. Retrieving expense entry data for a specific Expense ID

You might notice that there is some entry data missing from the request above, specifically, entry custom field data. In order to see the custom field data for an expense entry, you will need to add the expenseID from the last request like this:

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/{{contextType}}/reports/{{reportID}}/expenses/{{expenseID}}

 

5. Updating expense report header data

Each report header contains a variety of different fields depending on how your Concur site is setup. For the purposes of this blog post, I will be updating a few different fields in my test report header seen in the screenshot below.

5a - Retrieve Unique User ID -

Before we make any updates to expense report data, we will need to retrieve the unique identifier of the SAP Concur user. The unique identifier will be used for the "userId" parameter and can be found using the Identity v4 API like this:

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/profile/identity/v4/Users?filter=userName eq "tricia.connolly@tconnolly.com"

 

*Now that you have the userId, we can start making updates!*

5b - Update Header Text Field - 

First, I want to update the free text "Business Purpose" field. To do this I will send the new business purpose value in the body along with the reportId and the userId that we retrieved in the previous steps like this:

 

PATCH https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/TRAVELER/reports/{{reportID}}

 

You can expect to receive a "204 No Content" response if the patch is successful.

5c - Update Header Connected List Field- 

Next, I want to make updates to the "Region" and "Department" custom list fields in my report header. To do this, I will follow the 3 steps below:

Step 1 - Get all list data to find the list ID of the list you want to update

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/list/v4/lists/

 

Step 2 - Find the list item ids for the items you want to update using the list ID from step 1

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/list/v4/lists/{{listID}}/items

 

Note - If you are updating a connected or multi-level list, you will also need to find the child list item IDs

 

GET https://{{dataCenterURI}}/list/v4/lists/{{listID}}/items/{{itemID}}/children

 

Step 3- Use the list item ID to update the custom list fields

 

PATCH https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/TRAVELER/reports/{{reportID}}

 

You can expect to receive a "204 No Content" response if the patch is successful.

6. Updating Expense Report Entry Fields

Updating field values for expense items is just like header level fields, but you will need to include the expenseID that we retrieved in example 3 above. In this example I'm going to update the expense entry expense type and  the transaction amount. You can find a list of expense types and their ids using  Expense Group Configurations v3.0.

 

PATCH https://{{dataCenterURI}}/expensereports/v4/users/{{userID}}/context/{{contextType}}/reports/{{reportID}}/expenses/{{expenseID}}

 

You can expect to receive a "204 No Content" response if the patch is successful.

Conclusion

Using the examples in this post you should be able to view and update existing expense report and expense entry data. I recommend using this post to complement Concurs existing API documentation. The following were referenced while making this post -

  • Expenses v4
  • Reports v4
  • Reports v3
  • List v4
  • Expense Group Configurations v3

Feel free to comment if you have any questions. Thanks!

entry.title: Advanced Shipping and Receiving – Go-live support insights in bgRFC based EWM-TM integration
entry.content:
Introduction

With the Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv. SR) functionality the integration of  SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for Transportation Management and SAP S/4HANA Supply Chain for Extended Warehouse Management is refactored, compared to the direct integration of SAP TM Freight Order to the SAP EWM Transportation Unit (see also the blog entry of Jörg Michaelis).

While in the integration scenario of TM Freight Order and EWM Transportation Unit the webservice interface “TransportationOrderLoadingAppointmentNotification” is used, for Adv. SR a direct integration of the EWM warehouse order documents and the TM Freight Unit is implemented. During the updates of the document's situations can occur in which an asynchronous processing of updates is necessary (for example the TM documents are locked exclusively by another process).
For these asynchronous updates TM facilitates the Background RFC (bgRFC) technology in combination with the TM trigger concept (for further details to the bgRFC concept, please refer to SAP Notes: “2930217 – bgRFC Concept and High Level Process in SAP Transportation Management”, “2526415 - Updated trigger concept using bgRFC processing” and “3125518 - bgRFC maintenance in the context of SAP Transportation Management”. For more general questions regarding the bgRFC configuration refer to SAP note “2309399 - How to use SBGRFCCONF effectively?”).

In SAP Transportation Management the trigger concept and the usage of bgRFC technology for asynchronous processes are used since TM 9.5 / S/4HANA 1709 (compare SAP note 2641251).
Both is not exclusive for the Advanced Shipping and Receiving processes. Non the less, during the past Go-Lives with the Adv. SR processes and the close integration of the TM documents to its predecessor documents in LE and EWM, the trigger processing has been more in focus (for more details on monitoring the bgRFC units in Transportation Management see the SCN blog Analyse bgRFC Units created by SAP TM application.).

As the understanding of the trigger concept and the processing of the triggers is now of more importance for the operations monitoring, I want to share some of the insights our CoE team has gathered in the past Go-Lives which we supported in this blog.

Disclaimer: The described concept is of the status in the S/4HANA 2022 release. Changes to the concept or the implementation might apply in subsequent releases.

 

BGRFC update concept in Adv. SR

During the process execution of the Advanced Shipping and Receiving in- or outbound process the TM Freight Unit has to be kept in synch with its predecessor documents. Depending on the integration scenario and process state this can be the MM/SD order documents, LE deliveries or EWM in-/outbound delivery order documents. If applicable and necessary, these updates are processed asynchronously. For these asynchronous updates TM utilizes bgRFC units which refer to specific TM triggers. Along the process execution different TM triggers are used for different update scenarios. This might be explained in more detail in another blog post.

All the different triggers which are used in the Adv. SR processes are stateless, i.e., the triggers themselves do not carry any information which change to the source document created the trigger for the update of the target document. Thus during the processing of the trigger the application will derive the updates from the predecessor document.

The picture below shows a detailed example to explain the concept: In the loading of a EWM Outbound Delivery Order (ODO) contains four Handling Units (HU). The Outbound Delivery Order refers to one Freight Unit in which the HUs are reflected as Package Items.
In the example the Freight Unit is locked exclusively by an End User and thus the loading information cannot be updated in the Freight Unit by the EWM loading process immediately. The loading process of each HU will create one bgRFC trigger, which will be reprocessed periodically using the bgRFC framework. The time between each reprocessing is derived by a S/4HANA TM specific application logic.

At the point of time when the first of the four triggers is being processed, the source document – the EWM ODO – already contains all the changes which lead to the creation of the bgRFC units. Thus once the first trigger is processed, the other three triggers are obsolete as the update to the Freight Unit is not derived from the trigger context (-> stateless 😉), but from the source document directly.

To that reason the application added some logic to identify situations in which the triggers are obsolete and then shortcuts the processing of the BGRFC units, to avoid overhead in the processing. The following picture shell illustrate that behavior:

When monitoring BGRFC interfaces for ASR processes it can be observed that a higher number of triggers queue up for one TM document – for example due to locked Freight Unit – and then suddenly all triggers are gone. That is exactly the situation described above that the first trigger brings all relevant updates to the TM Freight Unit und thus the processing of the subsequent triggers is not applying any changes and thus consumes significantly less time.

Some technical details on the update process

Regarding the above described process some questions might come up, like:

  • Where are the TM Triggers defined?
  • How is the delay time between the reprocessing of the bgRFC units calculated?
  • How does the application determine when a trigger is obsolete?

Regarding the definition of the relevant TM Triggers we have to take a look at the BOPF object /SCMTMS/LINC. This object has been introduced with the Adv. SR processes. The “Logistics Integration Controller” object contains business logic for the integration of the TM Freight Unit to its predecessor documents.
Each Action on the ROOT node of the /SCMTMS/LINC object corresponds to one of the triggers used in the Adv. SR processes.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

As described at the beginning for one TM document multiple triggers of the same type can exist for the same S/4HANA TM document in parallel. During creation of the BGRFC unit also an instance on the ACTIVITY node of the /SCMTMS/LINC object is created.
Via the root node information of the document ID (BTD_ID) and the document type code (BTD_TCO) the Activity is linked to the predecessor document. The activity itself contains the information for which trigger type it has been created via the TRIGGER_ID field. The processing status indicates if the relevant change is scheduled (value “S”) or already processed (value “P”).

To identify which document type code is referring to which document type the attribute “C_TBD_TCO” of constants interface /SCMTMS/IF_COMMON_C can be checked, as shown in the screenshot below on the example of the EWM ODO, which has the document type code “496”.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

In the above provided example of the loading process the relevant BOPF action is “SET_TOR_STATUS”. Within the implementation it is checked if for the particular trigger a scheduled activity exists.

The screenshots show that in case no scheduled activity node can be found, the processing logic of the BOPF action is not executed.
After successful processing of the LINC action, also the activity node is updated.

Thus, after successful processing the status of the relevant node instances on the activity node of the LINC object will be updated from “S – Scheduled” to “P – Processed”. When the next trigger is processed and no open activity for this trigger type is found, the trigger will be processed without applying the entire application logic.

BGRFC Wait Times

In the beginning of the blog, it has been mentioned that the time between the reprocessing of a bgRFC unit in S/4HANA TM is derived by an application specific logic. The application specific logic is based on the retries of the individual BGRFC unit. The logic is implemented in class /SCMTMS/CL_TRIG_HELPER. 

Besides the wait time also the maximum number of retries per bgRFC unit is handled in the application specific logic. The default retry count is set via the member variable of class /SCMTMS/CL_TRIG_HELPER and is defaulted as 256 retries.

The bgRFC units in S/4HANA TM are processed for 2 days with an implemented cool down mechanism so that units which could not be processed successfully will have increasing delays between reprocessing.

Summary

The provided example should give a brief explanation how in the Advanced Shipping and Receiving processes the stateless updates between EWM and TM are processed using the bgRFC concept.
The example briefly explains the application specific calculation of the reprocessing wait times for the bgRFC units in Transportation Management.

Having worked through go-lives of interfaces which used sequenced messages that had to be processed exactly once and in the right order to ensure a consistent document status along the document chain, I found the stateless trigger concept quiet handy since I did not need to care in which sequence the messages needed to be processed.
Furthermore in the Adv. SR. process a single failed bgRFC unit does not block the entire queue, so that a unit which for example failed due to a temporary inconsistency does not necessarily block the entire communication between EWM and TM for the affected documents.
At the same time when I first worked with this concept in scenarios with many updates happening in a short period of time I needed a moment to get used to the idea that it does not matter which trigger was created by which change or in which sequence the trigger needed to be processed.

I hope the provided example helped you to understand the advantage of the stateless trigger concept, the calculation of the wait time between the reprocessing of the bgRFC units and the mechanisms implemented in the application to avoid unnecessary trigger processing.

 

Learn more about SAP Premium Engagement and how a global team of experts can help you to get the most out of your SAP solutions.

If you already are a Premium Engagement customer, contact your Technical Quality Manager (TQM) to discuss how Premium Engagement services can help you with a custom-tailored and efficient service portfolio to make your project a success!

#CoE EMEA SCM1

entry.title: Inventory Stock Data from SAP Business 1 with PHP Curl.
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I'm reaching out for assistance on retrieving stock inventory data using PHP and cURL. My goal is to obtain the most up-to-date inventory stock, including product SKUs, from the SAP Business One application and then update it within Magento 2 products. While I've successfully set up the Magento 2 API, I'm currently stumped on how to extract all product inventory data from SAP.

Any guidance or insights on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Are there any recommendations / restriction of number...
entry.content: you don't have to create big cds, just create star model(fact+dimension).

entry.title: Re: ABAP DDIC names of CDS Views
entry.content: why not open the cds yourself?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2021 – Feature Pack Stack 2 (FPS02)
entry.content:
Dear Christian,

I'm in the middle of implementation of Health & Safety Management with Release S/4HANA2021 FPS02, and I'm currently doing The Migration with a self-developed Program, since no standard migration object exists. It works fine except for Migration of P-Statements, Where P-statements are not available in SAP chemical Management App "Manage Chemicals" There is no field, where I can put the P-Statements out of the migration file?Thank you for Feedback

Kind regards

Gaber Mahmoud

entry.title: Re: Navigation menu color
entry.content: I agree that the ability to customize the menu used to exist.  As you can see below in the picture, I only have one option which is the reason for my post.

entry.title: Datasphere - shared view is not available in Database
entry.content:
Hi ,

I shared a view from SPACE A to SPACE B in datasphere. The view is accessible for the users in Space B application layer but not visible in database for the SPACE B database user.

Is there any way to make view available in database or it's just a limitation in SAP datasphere ?

Please share your thougths

Thanks,

Venky

 

SAP Datasphere

I tried to find a way to connect via VBSscritp with SSO, but I couldn't find anything.

entry.content:
When creating the BOM, why are there two methods (CS01 vs C201)?  What are the advantages/disadvantages of one over the other?  

Are there some example scenarios that can help me understand how these are applied and how to decide between which one to use?

entry.title: Re: Create Z FIORI Catalog & Z Group, Mapping with User Profile & Assign Missing Authorizati
entry.content:
not bad.

group is deprecated now. you should learn how to use space

entry.title: Re: ABAP DDIC names of CDS Views
entry.content:
Hi Tom,

 

as already mentioned by Sandra, you can do that yourself.

 

I still quickly did it for you:

I_MfgOrderItemSerialNumber -> IPPMFGORDITMSERN

I_ManufacturingOrder -> IMFGORDER

I_MfgOrderWithStatus -> IMFGORDERWSTTS

I_SerialNumberManufacturingOrd -> this is not a CDS view but an entity view?

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: Re: YTD|MTD|QTD in SAP HANA CDs Views using date function
entry.content:
it is adt, not hana studio

your screenshot is too small, not really visible...

entry.title: Dynamic field labels sap rap
entry.content:
Hello,

We have created an app in BTP environment using Restful ABAP.  Since this app will be used by various different customers, we would like to make the field labels configurable as each customer might want to name these fields differently.

Please let me know if this is achievable.  Is there a way we can pass the annotations dynamically ?

thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: AI chatbot
entry.content: Hi Naseer,  With AI you can not just search but can get a instant knowledge created from various bits of knowledge across documents.  Feel free to email me at kn@ascendo.ai or connect on Linkedin

entry.title: Re: Export PDF via API Gateway for Unicode (Khmer Fonts)...
entry.content:
HI @ANKIT_CHAUHAN

I am using SAP HANA, I have been checking this before.
3367780 - Letters Appear as Question Marks When Converting a Base64 Encoded String to PDF Using API Gateway - SAP for Me
and it work but the character display does not correct as I mentioned above.

(SAP HANA) I think problem is about Registry on SAP HANA that running on Linux SUSE. 

For Window (It is work fined)

  • Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00
  • [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Crystal Reports\Export]
  • [HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Crystal Reports\Export\PDF]
    "ForceLargerFonts"=dword:00000001

Is there any configuration to SAP HANA?

entry.title: Re: Failed to resolve endpoint: xslt://pd:HCIOWNERPID:MAP...
entry.content: I really appreciate your solution

entry.title: Received stock does not have inspection ID in ARM
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm running ARM process in the test system. I've created a return order and followed the return delivery.

After the stock of returned delivery has been GR and putaway to the clarification zone, I found there is no inspection ID for the stock. I'm wonder if there is something needs to be configured, thanks a lot.

Regards,
Eric

entry.title: Re: What is the difference between creating BOM in CS01 v...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP PP process manufacturing it is possible to maintain BOM, production version and recipe using C201, which makes master data maintenance easier.

If BOMs to be maintained separately or by a separate user role, it can be isolated to T code CS01.

entry.title: Re: Unable to download in DMS using CAPM NodeJS
entry.content: Thank you so much for helping. I found the solution it was CAP interpreting any retrieved file as UTF-8 while this is not always matching the codeset of our file. So we have to use SAP Cloud SDK to bypass this UTF-8 conversion as it allows us to specify the Response Type to be the raw buffer which was also present within the plugin you used

entry.title: Re: Same Mapping Different WSDL
entry.content: deleted

entry.title: Re: Steps for SAP Companion for SAP S/4HANA On-Premise mi...
entry.content:
Ahoy matey!

I have 2 options for you:

  1. You can find the SAP Companion for SAP S/4HANA On-premise setup instructions in the SAP Companion Integration Guide for versions 2305 or older. Catch the direct link. 
  2. In the SAP Companion Integration Guide for SAP Enable Now 2305, on the page Setup of SAP Companion for SAP Systems with ABAP Back End it is mentioned that the instructions can be found in the Installation Guide for SAP S/4HANA on their Product Page. Here is a direct link to this guide. 

P.S. It wasn't me who decided to do this, so don't shoot the messenger 💥.

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion supported applications ????
entry.content:
Catch the guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ENABLE_NOW/de298b01689a443a9afcedbbee576276/8846a96defa24795a97db8211b47c024.html?locale=en-US&version=2311 

If you can't find the application in the table, them in 95% of cases if the app utilizes SAP Fiori Launchpad, then the SAP Companion can be implemented.

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry - Change (Asynchronous) to update CD amount
entry.content: Hi All, I am still finding a solution to this. how we can update CD amount using Journal Entry - Change (Asynchronous).

entry.title: Re: Journal Entry - Change (Asynchronous) to update CD am...
entry.content:
"i believe you have tested the extent of the API" - using extensibility, we can't change the standard field: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/3ab6e6fc510f4840a5508e126ef01e22/c3833c31e7724a79b23e4273290f3e2e.html?locale=en-US#extensibility

I am looking for a solution to how we can update the CD amount using standard API.

entry.title: SAP inventory reports
entry.content:
Hi all, I'm hoping that someone will be able to help me out with inventory reports in SAP? I use 2 of them currently (daily cycle counts and to generate picklists) but I'm wondering if there's a report that will provide a list of all items in the store that are not currently in stock (not negatives, but zero)?

What I want to do is take that list and check whether those items are actually zero or if their numbers need to be adjusted.

Any help would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Sap Fiori Element Composition of one issues when usin...
entry.content: True. The issue in Fiori Tools is not because of missing key for MachineDimensionLimits. Tools is expecting a to-many association at the node itself. But, missing key will be a problem at runtime. Even when this key is added, one lands up in another issue in CAP; creation at path involving to-one composite associations is currently problematic. So, currently working scenario is when the to-one association limitsGroupVersion is merged like Ajit suggests.

entry.title: Wrong inspection plan got assigned to material in inspection lot
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

I am facing a problem, where the inspection lot got created for a material with REL status, but the inspection plan was not created for it. In fact an another material inspection plan got assigned to it. Can you please let me know, where the issue is.

Regards

Pankaj Verma

Is it possible?

Regards.

entry.title: Re: EWM 1809 - RF - Replenishment for Fixed Bin
entry.content:
Hello,

I also faced the same issue and it was solved after assinging default party entitled to dispose at warehouse level.

Thanks,

Saurabh Nigade

entry.title: Re: Se perdió la conexión con la base de datos; comuníquese con el administrador del sistema (ODBC-1
entry.content: Buenas noches, No se si este tema fue cerrado, yo encontré la solución, tengo SAP B1 con SQL, lo que deben hacer es agregar el usuario B1XXXXXXXXRW (x=CODIGO DE USUARIO) y asignarle el permiso de owner y puclic a la base de datos que restauraron, normalmente este error se da en este proceso.

entry.title: Re: Download File from DMS using Custom API

entry.title: Re: Calling a SAP PI/PO RFC integration from Java with JCo Library

Thank you for this very, very helpful blog!

Do you possibly happen to know, how to activate SNC in a scenario like the one you described?

Thanks, Nick

entry.title: ABAP RAP error " Handler not implemented; Method: INSTANCE_FEATURE "
entry.content:
Hello Fellow Experts,

I am currently implementing a custom development using ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model on our client's SAP On-Premise environment and building a Fiori App.

I am trying to implement a Dynamic Feature Control using Behavior Definition but doing so results in this error when accessing the Fiori App: 

Looking further here is the Dump 

 

 

However I seem to have implemented the code properly as here is my Behavior Definition

And even have the handler as the Dump claims is not yet created. 

But on debugging it doesn't even reach this part of the code.

I tried this development in our internal SAP Sandbox environment and it worked. I debugged both systems and the difference I noticed is when calling the class 

CL_ABAP_BEHV_FJOB->GET_BEHAVIOR  the method CL_ABAP_BEHV_CTRL=>GET_IMPLEMENTATIONS_KRN is being called to get the appropriate Behaviors (BDI_OPS)

BDI_OPS doesn't return a record with OPERATION = I ( for Instance) only Operation S. Our Sandbox environment picks up both records at this point.

I checked the Behavior Definition (D010BEHV) table and this record exists:

I even added the entry in debugger and I was able to get to the Handler Method and it was working fine. So, for some reason, at this point of the program this particular record isn't getting picked up. I am not sure why as everything was working in our other Sandbox SAP system.

Unfortunately this is as far as I can go the method CL_ABAP_BEHV_CTRL=>GET_IMPLEMENTATIONS_KRN is a Kernel Module so I can check any closer.

If anyone has an idea what is wrong here, I would greatly appreciate some assistance.

Thanks everyone in advanced

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
hi David ,

like many others i started out with a Windows IT field and lately i discovered Opensap Elearning , never had any experience with ERP before so i find it very interesting to learn and discover some news .

I challenge my self to do one or 2 Sap certification in the next 90 days . It s good to have a goal in the future .

 

Best regards

entry.title: Material Number Ranges
entry.content:
I wish to assign existing intervals to the group for a material type. I tried doing the same, but it says the intervals overlap and I cannot save the group. Please help me assigning the existing intervals if that is possible. 

Thanks,

Akash

entry.title: Re: PO order history report
entry.content: how to find Purchase order history in sap b1....??

entry.title: Re: CDS Annotations: Activate DraftPersistence without Ac...
entry.content:
Hi,

It's possible, but it's hard to find documentation for that. In 7.50 this topic is limited. SAP delivers the Fiori-App 'Manage Products' as part of the EPM-Model. This app has draft capabilities. 

Try somthing like this

@ObjectModel: {

modelCategory: #BUSINESS_OBJECT,

compositionRoot: true,

semanticKey: [ '<key>' ],

writeEnabled: true,

writeDraftPersistence: '<draft table>'

}



Bets regrads
Stefa

Please help here

entry.title: Re: API_SALES_ORDER retuning empty values for sales order...
entry.content:
Hi Abdul,

 

have you checked afterward on the database if the sales order was created with the items?

If yes then it might be that the creation happens asynchronously. Otherwise, which I am guessing right now a bit more, you did not create the items on the salesorder. You have not done a deep insert and therefore the items are empty.

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Financials Development

entry.title: GRC Process control jobs are not able to upload data in customized tables from plug in system to GRC
entry.content: Process control CCM jobs were running fine but suddenly something happened and now jobs are not taking/uploading data in GRC system from Plug in system. What can be the reason of this. Data is there in plug in system but not uploading in customized tables.

entry.title: Re: BAPI for PA40 Create HR Mini Master Data
entry.content:
Hi Leonard, if you Search with "PA40 Bapi" in the community search field you will find as second entry the answer. The first entry will be your question,

Best Regards

Bernd

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 1 - S/4HANA OData
entry.content:
Here my answer on a sunny day in BE 😀

3bc3d7b9eb5b9947c6d5a560c24da4513a7796f86a5285facac8e2f2cd6ef4aa

SAP Build Codejam
